page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not feature any GPU acceleration.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.n2.large sits primarily in the lower tiers for compute and memory operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the instance in the bottom 25 percent, with scores of 1089.08 and 4315.32 respectively. Memory bandwidth read-only tests also fall into the bottom 25 percent, though large-block write bandwidth reaches an average tier at 28.16 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks show poor performance, landing in the bottom 10 percent. Passmark CPU extended instructions test shows average performance at 5297.22, while database operations and Redis SET operations remain in the bottom 25 percent.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.n2.large is its lower-tier processing and memory performance relative to modern hardware classes, combined with the complete absence of local storage. However, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent, non-burstable performance. This instance is suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale database operations, and development environments that do not require high-throughput memory or intensive computational power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and no local storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation but exhibits lower-tier benchmark results. Stress-ng compute and Passmark memory scores place the instance in the bottom 25 percent of tested hardware, though it achieves average performance in large-block memory write bandwidth and extended CPU instructions. Lacking GPU acceleration and local storage, the ecs.n2.large is designed for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and development environments requiring predictable, non-burstable CPU resources without intensive computational demands.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering entry-level compute performance for basic workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and KVM-based virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring four Intel Xeon vCPUs and sixteen gigabytes of memory for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM per core
KVM hypervisor architecture with dedicated CPU allocation and no hyperthreading
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the default configuration
Average performance in large-block memory write and extended CPU instruction benchmarks
Lower-tier performance in single-core compute, database operations, and memory read bandwidth
Suitable for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic database tasks
